original: Three astronauts simultaneously walk in space for the first time
New study on the moon shows it is still shrinking with recent moonquakes as it cools, published in journal "Nature Geoscience"
World's largest iceberg 'A-76' at 1,667-square-miles (4,320 square km) calves off the Ronne Ice Shelf in Antarctica
Hail stones as large as tennis balls hit Wernerville, Tennessee
Joie Chitwood drives a Chevette 5.6 miles on just 2 wheels
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's first opera "Apollo et Hyacinthus", written when he was 11 years old, premieres in Salzburg
22-year-old UK blogger halts the spread of a global ransomware cyberattack by accidentally identifying a "kill switch"
Aviation pioneerIgor Sikorskypilots first untethered test flight of his VS-300 helicopter in Stratford, Connecticut
Ritchie Valens [Valenzuela], American rock singer ("Donna"; "La Bamba"), born in Pacoima, California (d. 1959)
Joe Johnston, American film director (Honey, I Shrunk The Kids; Jumanji; October Sky), and visual effects artist, born in Austin, Texas
Octagonal boxing ring is tested to avoid corner injuries
DeWolf Hopper first recites the comedic baseball poem "Casey at the Bat"
Cincinnati Red Ray Knight hits 2 HRs in 5th inning vs NY Mets
Ron Necciai of Pittsburgh Pirate's Bristol Twins Class D farm team, strikes out 27 in 9-innings, as he no-hits Welch Minors; 4 Minors do reach base
Canadian astronautChris Hadfielddeparts the International Space Station to return to Earth
Claudie Haigneré, French astronaut (Soyuz TM-23, TM-24, TM-32, TM-33) and first female French astronaut, born in Le Creusot, France[1]
Princess Isabel of Brazil acting as regent signs the "Lei Auréa" (Golden Law) abolishing slavery in Brazil
Portuguese children Francisco and Jacinta Marto (died 1919 & 1920 aged 10 & 9 in flu epidemic) canonized byPope Francisat Fátima - the youngest Catholic saints not to die as martyrs
Trevor Baylis, English inventor of the wind-up radio, born in Kilburn, London (d. 2018)
The Great Comet of 1861 is discovered by John Tebbutt of Windsor, New South Wales, Australia
The First Fleet, headed byArthur Phillip, sets sail with 11 ships of convicts for Botany Bay, Australia
English colonists led byJohn Smithestablish Jamestown at a second landing near the James River in Virginia - first permanent English settlement in North America
First flight of a four-engined aircraft, the Russky Vityaz, designed byIgor Sikorskyin Russia
Helicopter makes its 1st cross-country flight
1st appearance of Mary to 3 shepherd children in Fatima, Portugal

Manchester City beats QPR 3-2 at City of Manchester Stadium to win English Premier League title on goal difference from Manchester United; Citizens recover from 2–1 down with 2 stoppage-time goals; first EPL title decided on goal difference
Manchester City ends the EPL season with a 1-0 win at Southampton having smashed 11 Premier League records including biggest title winning margin (19 points) and most points (100)
Liverpool's Egyptian soccer forwardMohamed Salahscores in a 4-0 win against Brighton to set the EPL goal scoring record (32) for a 38-game season
Chance the Rapperreleases his third mixtape "Coloring Book" on Apple Music - first album to chart on Billboard Top 100 based only on streams (Grammy for Best Rap Album)
MLB Cleveland Indians begin a 18 home game win streak at newly opened Jacobs Field
MLB San Francisco Giants teammatesWillie Mays& Darryl Spencer each hit 2 HRs & a triple, and combine for 10 RBI in a 16-9 win over the Dodgers at Los Angeles

Commissioner Rob Manfred removes 17 deceased people, including "Shoeless" Joe Jackson andPete Rosefrom MLB's 'permanently ineligible' list stating, “Obviously, a person no longer with us cannot represent a threat to the integrity of the game,” paving the way for HOF inclusion
Bruce Logan, British cinematographer and visual effects artist (Star Wars: Episode IV — A New Hope; 2001: A Space Odyssey), born in London (d. 2025)
Vladimir Dzhanibekov, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 27, 39, T-6, T-12, T-13), born in Iskandar, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union
Soyuz T-5 is launched-Berezovoi & Lebedev for 211 days in space
Unidentified shipwreck is discovered off the northern coast of Haiti by marine archaeologist Barry Clifford
1st observance of Indian (Native American) Day
The trade mark Velcro is registered
World heavyweight boxing championJames J. Jeffriesretires undefeated after 7 title defences; returns in 1910 to be beaten by Jack Johnson
Eddie Murrayis 6th baseball player to play in 3,000 games
Yankee Stadium fans sing "Like a Virgin" to JoseCanseco
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,Bruce Springsteen,Mary J. Blige,Debbie Harry,Lady Gaga,Shirley Bassey, Nile Rodgers and Chic
Anne Merriman, British doctor who revolutionized palliative care in Uganda, born in Liverpool, England (d. 2025)[1]
Every African country records cases of COVID-19 as The Kingdom of Lesotho becomes the last country on the continent to record a case
Torrential rain in Hunan Province, China, destroys a bridge, 3,500 homes and displaces 28,000 people
China's first domestically built aircraft carrier begins sea trials in Dalian, China
DutchQueen Wilhelminaflees to England
Maria Theresa, Holy Roman Empress, Archduchess of Austria, Queen of Hungary and Bohemia (1740-1780) only woman to rule Habsburg Empire in her own right, born in Vienna (d. 1780)
Construction of the Red Fort begins at Shahjahanabad, Mughal Empire (now Dehli, India)
First-ever race of the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship is run at Silverstone, England, and won by Giuseppe Farina of Italy in an Alfa Romeo
Georges Braque, French cubist painter and sculptor (My bicycle) who helped develop Cubism with Pablo Picasso, born in Argenteuil, Val-d'Oise (d. 1963)
Daphne du Maurier, English writer (Rebecca, Parasites), born in London, England (d. 1989)
Aleksandr Kaleri, Russian cosmonaut (Soyuz TM-14), born in Jūrmala, Latvian SSR, Soviet Union
Vitaly Dubko, Russian trampolining coach (named best coach of 20th Century by International Gymnastics Federation), born in Krasnodar, Russia (d. 2023)
T. Balasaraswati, Indian Bharatanatyam dancer, born in Madras Presidency, British India (d. 1984)
Bob Dalton, American outlaw of the Old West (leader of the Dalton Gang), born in Cass County, Missouri (d. 1892)
Inge Lehmann, Danish geophysicist and seismologist, born in Østerbro, Denmark (d. 1993)
Francine Pascal, American author of the "Sweet Valley High" book series, born in Manhattan (d. 2024)[1]
Roger Zelazny, American poet and sci-fi author (Chronicles of Amber), born in Euclid, Ohio (d. 1995)
Mary Wells, American pop singer ("My Guy";"You Beat Me to the Punch"), born in Detroit, Michigan (d. 1992)
